steps.action: Look at the bottom of the screen
steps.targetDesc: The Dock — the bar of app icons at the screen's bottom edge
steps.expectedState: The Dock is visible with application icons displayed in a row
steps.why: Locating the Dock is the entry point to launching any app quickly
steps.pitfall: Don't confuse the Dock with the Menu Bar at the very top of the screen

steps.targetDesc: A blue compass-style circular icon labeled "Safari" in the Dock
steps.expectedState: The icon highlights or shows a tooltip with the app name on hover
steps.why: Identifying the correct icon prevents accidentally opening the wrong browser
steps.pitfall: Don't mistake Chrome or Firefox for Safari — Safari's icon is a compass with a blue/white design

steps.targetDesc: Single left-click on the Safari compass icon in the Dock
steps.expectedState: The icon bounces briefly and Safari launches, opening a new browser window
steps.why: A single click is all that's needed to launch a Dock app
steps.pitfall: Avoid double-clicking — this may open two Safari windows simultaneously

steps.action: Wait for Safari to fully load
steps.targetDesc: The Safari window with an address bar, tab bar, and start page
steps.expectedState: Safari's start page (or your homepage) is visible and interactive
steps.why: Waiting ensures Safari is ready before you interact with it
steps.pitfall: Don't click repeatedly if it seems slow — it just needs a moment to initialize
